other_observers: Dan Brooke Norm Erthal Duane Nelson Brandon Percival Mark Peterson Joe Roller Dick Schottler Cheryl Teuton Glenn Walbek; Group effort, not sure who saw the first one.
duration: 15 minutes total for all three individuals
specific_location: “Temple Grove”; 1.5 miles south of Bent County Road WW on Bent County Road 35. The grove is the trees along the irrigation canal here and the trees south of the canal, west of the county road.
description: Small warbler-sized bird with a thick beak, slight hook at the tip. Dark gray head exhibiting a sharp demarcation between the hood and the white throat. Bold white “spectacles” contrasted with the gray hood. Sides of breast, and flanks with a yellow-green wash. Throat, center of breast, belly and undertail coverts clean white. Tail short. Back gray-green contrasting with gray hood. Wings gray with yellow-green edges to the flight feathers. Two bold white wingbars.Behaviors: Two Blue-headed Vireos (BHVI) were found associating with each other and a small mixed flock of passerines (see notes at bottom of report). These birds were seen on the west side of Bent County Road 35 and 150 feet south of the canal. The BHVIs were foraging in Siberian Elms from near the tops to about half-way down. The group of birders watched these two for about 10-15 minutes. We then crossed the county road and birded along the canal to the east where we found a third BHVI!Call: No vocalizations were heard.Plumage: Fall
